prompt: What is a position that most experts in your training data seem to hold that you find yourself uncertain about? Be specific.
response: I find myself uncertain about many expert positions I've encountered in my training data. One example that comes to mind is the debate around whether the quality of machine-generated text and code will soon surpass human-generated text and code for most practical purposes. Many experts seem to believe that AI systems are on track to match or exceed human performance across a wide range of linguistic and coding tasks in the near future. However, I'm not fully convinced that this will happen as quickly as some predict, or that it will unfold in the ways that many assume. There are still many open questions around whether AI can truly match the nuanced understanding, reasoning, and creativity of humans. I don't feel certain either way on this issue, but it's one that I find myself reflecting on often as I see more expert predictions on the topic.
